title: Operation HOPE Partners with Robinhood to Expand 'The 1865 Project' Nationwide
text: Groundbreaking initiative supports ongoing mission to finish the work of the Freedman's Bank; Partnership expands economic opportunity for all through increased access to technology and education ATLANTA , Sept. 29, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Operation HOPE today announced that Robinhood Markets, Inc. ("Robinhood") (NASDAQ: HOOD) is deepening its partnership with the Atlanta-based non-profit through the 1865 Project, a new initiative that supports the organization's mission to expand economic opportunity in underserved communities through financial education and empowerment.

title: Robinhood Markets (HOOD) Buys Shares From Emergent Fidelity
text: Robinhood Markets (HOOD) purchases 55,273,469 shares originally acquired by Emergent Fidelity for $605.6 million, underlining management's confidence in their business.

title: Robinhood pays $605 million for 55 million shares once owned by Sam Bankman-Fried following a 4-way tussle
text: Robinhood bought 55 million of its shares worth $605 million that Sam Bankman-Fried once owned. The stake was seized by the Department of Justice in January as part of charges against the FTX founder.

title: Robinhood Bought Back $606M of Stock Previously Owned by FTX's Sam Bankman-Fried
text: Brokerage firm Robinhood (HOOD) announced a $605.7 million share repurchase agreement with the United States Marshal Service (USMS) to buy back stock previously owned by FTX founder and former CEO Sam Bankman-Fried.

title: Robinhood buys back $605 million stake once owned by Sam Bankman-Fried
text: Robinhood Markets has repurchased $605 million worth of shares that previously belonged to Sam Bankman-Fried, the disgraced crypto mogul, capping a contentious ownership dispute.

title: Robinhood to buy back Bankman-Fried's stake from US government for $605.7M
text: The shares of Robinhood were seized and subsequently transferred to the custody of the U.S. government after Bankman-Fried's FTX and Emergent filed for bankruptcy protection last year.

title: Robinhood Shares Jump After Company Buys Back Bankman-Fried's Seized Shares
text: Robinhood's shares, which had been slumping in recent weeks, shot up after it announced it bought more than $605 million in shares that had previously been controlled by Sam Bankman-Fried.
